title: fix(quest): resolve PR→quest link via GraphQL, ping fallback on miss
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
## Summary
- Replace body-keyword regex with `pullRequest.closingIssuesReferences` so the resolver also catches PRs linked via the GitHub Development sidebar — maintainers can rescue a quest PR pre-merge without retraining contributors.
- On miss (no `POLLEN-QUEST` linked at merge), post a comment on the PR pinging \`QUEST_PAYOUT_FALLBACK\` instead of silently green-skipping the payout. Fixes the failure mode from PR #10927 / quest #10920.

## Why
PR #10927 fulfilled quest #10920 but said \"Goes with #10920\" — not a closing keyword. The current regex missed it, Compute/Grant/Receipt all skipped, workflow ended green, contributor only noticed hours later. Three independent design passes (research survey, Plan, Codex) agreed: GitHub's native PR↔issue link is strictly stronger than parsing contributor free text.

## Test plan
- [x] \`Fixes #N\` in body → resolves (same as today)
- [x] Sidebar-linked issue, no keyword → resolves (new)
- [x] No link at all → no payout, fallback maintainer pinged on the PR (was: silent skip)
- [x] \`npx biome check --write\` clean

## Notes
- Workflow gains \`pull-requests: write\` for the miss-comment (was \`read\`).
- Output contract of \`resolveLinkedQuest\` is unchanged — \`computePayout\` / \`runPollenGrant\` / \`postReceipt\` are untouched.
- CloudCompile's 5 Pollen for #10920 has been back-filled manually with \`quest-grant-pollen.ts\`.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

gist.headline: No quest left behind
gist.blurb: We've upgraded our quest payout system to use GitHub's native issue linking, ensuring contributors get their Pollen even if they forget the magic 'Fixes #' keyword.
gist.summary: The quest reward payout workflow now uses GitHub's GraphQL API to resolve linked issues instead of regex parsing, and pings maintainers if a PR is merged without a linked quest.
gist.impact: Contributors will reliably receive their Pollen rewards when linking quests via the GitHub sidebar, and maintainers will be alerted to manually intervene if a link is missing.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee acting as a postal worker or detective, successfully connecting a PR document to a quest board. The robot companion helps ensure nothing gets lost.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly the bee wearing a tiny postal worker hat, happily pinning a glowing document to a wooden quest board. A chunky retro robot with a CRT monitor head gives a thumbs up. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley, chunky sprites.
